subject: UX & Research
title: Microsoft's Inclusive Design Toolkit
description: A 60-second lesson on Microsoft's Inclusive Design Toolkit.

hook.text: A person with one arm and a person holding a baby have the same need. Microsoft built a toolkit for this.

buildup.text: The toolkit defines three disability types: permanent (one arm), temporary (broken arm), situational (holding baby).

discovery.text: Designing for permanent disability solves temporary and situational needs too. One solution, three groups.

twist.text: Xbox's adaptive controller was designed for disabled gamers. Parents with babies also use it one-handed.

climax.text: Microsoft's approach: start with exclusion, find the spectrum, and design for the edges to help the center.

punchline.text: Permanent, temporary, situational — same need, one solution. Design for edges.

quiz.question: What are the three types of disability in Microsoft's Inclusive Design Toolkit?
options: Permanent, temporary, and situational — all creating the same functional need
